action_metadata.metadata_title: Bifrost: Unlocking Bitcoin DeFi on Cardano — Road to Mainnet (Phase 1 of 2)

onchain_context:
onchain DRep vote counts so far: yes=20 no=5 abstain=4 (explicit ballots, not stake-weighted)
treasury flow (36 epochs): inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492

fact_verdicts.fact: Action id is dfd81f8db652fd9263e0ce3bef043b4823045ec3a5da10c817681ada7a23f034#0; action type is TreasuryWithdrawals; proposed epoch is 640; expires after epoch 647. [onchain: action header]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 0 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The action header supplied to this dossier lists treasury amount (lovelace) as "(none)". [onchain: action header]
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 0 lists treasury amount (lovelace): (none).

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor title is "Bifrost: Unlocking Bitcoin DeFi on Cardano — Road to Mainnet (Phase 1 of 2)". [anchor: body.title]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor body.title.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says FluidTokens and Lantr Engineering request ₳12,332,031, approximately $1,973,125 at 0.16 USD/ADA, including a 10% refundable contingency, for a 9-month delivery period from July 2026 to March 2027. [anchor: abstract; anchor: Detailed Budget]
fact_verdicts.note: The abstract and Detailed Budget state these amounts, rate, contingency, and delivery period.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says Phase 1 funds work to take Bifrost from working testnet to launch readiness, including hardening, security audits, formal verification, ecosystem and partner readiness, and stewardship/economic foundations. [anchor: abstract]
fact_verdicts.note: The abstract states these Phase 1 work areas.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says public rollout and 24 months of operations are separated into a Phase 2 proposal in Q1 2027 after the bridge has been proven on-chain. [anchor: abstract; anchor: Phase 2 - Adoption plan]
fact_verdicts.note: The abstract separates public rollout and 24 months of operations into Phase 2 in Q1 2027 once proven on-chain.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says Bifrost is currently funded through Catalyst Fund 14 for ₳739,000, with deliverables of Architecture, Testnet MVP, and Documentation, and a timeline of Dec 2025 - Aug 2026 marked on track. [anchor: Catalyst Fund 14 table]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Catalyst Fund 14 table.

fact_verdicts.fact: Catalyst milestone status in the anchor lists M1 and M2 as done/approved, M3 as submitted awaiting Catalyst signoff, M4 and M5 on track for July 2026, and final reporting/full end-to-end execution on track for August 2026. [anchor: Catalyst milestone status]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Catalyst milestone status table.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says the bridge is live and operational on testnet, with peg-in between Bitcoin Testnet4 and Cardano Preprod fully working and open to anyone, and gives transaction proof a43ce4586939509d0845bb9cf1e5a9b6242faca2b977d31b484d225b9c9ae6db. [anchor: Testnet live today]
fact_verdicts.note: The Testnet live today section states this and gives that transaction proof.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor names five SPOs as testnet volunteers: BTBF, ADA North Pool, BCSH, EASY1, DAVE. [anchor: Working with SPOs]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the listed testnet volunteer SPOs.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor names Cardano applications that expressed interest in integrating Bifrost or fBTC: Minswap, SundaeSwap, Masumi, Gravity, DeltaDefi, Liqwid, FluidTokens, Vela Finance. [anchor: Cardano dApps engagement]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the named applications in the anchor.

fact_verdicts.fact: The Phase 1 budget overview lists Bridge hardening & Security at $1,363,750 / ₳8,523,438 / 76.0%; Ecosystem readiness & partnerships at $177,500 / ₳1,109,375 / 9.9%; Legal, stewardship & economy at $252,500 / ₳1,578,125 / 14.1%; subtotal $1,793,750 / ₳11,210,938; refundable contingency $179,375 / ₳1,121,094; total $1,973,125 / ₳12,332,031. [anchor: Budget overview]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Phase 1 budget overview table.

fact_verdicts.fact: The detailed budget lists 52.5 FTE-months, fixed USD of $875,000, subtotal $1,793,750, refundable contingency $179,375, and total $1,973,125 / ₳12,332,031. [anchor: Detailed Budget]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Detailed Budget table totals.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states engineering and product capacity are priced at $210,000 per FTE-year, described as $100/hour blended rate for senior DLT engineers and product leadership. [anchor: Pricing principles]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Pricing principles section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says unspent contingency is returned to the Cardano Treasury at the end of the term. [anchor: Detailed Budget]
fact_verdicts.note: The Detailed Budget section states unspent contingency is returned at the end of the term.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says a portion of requested ADA will be hedged into stable assets shortly after disbursement to protect delivery and security audit budgets against adverse price movement. [anchor: Pricing principles]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Pricing principles section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor defines three Phase 1 milestones: M1 Q3 2026 Hardening & Audit Readiness, M2 Q4 2026 Security Audits & Mainnet Preparation, and M3 Q1 2027 Private Mainnet — Audited & Running. [anchor: Milestones and deliverables]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Milestones and deliverables section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says M3 acceptance criteria include published off-chain audit and pentest reports, public bug bounty, published mainnet contract addresses, successful private mainnet peg-in and peg-out in both federated and SPO threshold modes with public transaction hashes, public transparency portal, stewardship charter, hardened economic model, and documented partnership pipeline. [anchor: M3 acceptance criteria]
fact_verdicts.note: The M3 acceptance criteria state all listed items, including public transaction hashes and charter publication.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says Bifrost has no bridge token, no founder allocation, is open source under Apache 2.0, reports operations/milestones/treasury disbursements/on-chain activity publicly by default, and plans independent stewardship. [anchor: Public infrastructure]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Public infrastructure section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says long-term stewardship will be an independent foundation or equivalent independent legal form, and Phase 1 commits to evaluate/select form and jurisdiction, stand up the legal entity, and publish its governance charter before Phase 2. [anchor: Ownership & stewardship]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Ownership & stewardship section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says the intended Phase 2 revenue waterfall is mechanism reserve, maintenance reserve, governance reserve, then surplus split between an SPO retainer pool and the Cardano Treasury with indicative default 35% / 65%, both in fBTC. [anchor: Revenue Model & Sustainability]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Revenue Model & Sustainability section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says fee rates, reserve target, and 35% / 65% surplus split are provisional or adjustable by governance, and exact reserve target is set during Phase 1 hardening. [anchor: Revenue Model & Sustainability]
fact_verdicts.note: The section says fee schedule is provisional, reserve target is adjusted/set during Phase 1, and the 35%/65% split is provisional and adjustable.

fact_verdicts.fact: On-chain DRep vote counts supplied so far are yes=20, no=5, abstain=4, explicit ballots not stake-weighted. [onchain: DRep vote counts]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The supplied treasury flow for 36 epochs is inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492. [onchain: treasury flow]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine prioritizes constitutional integrity/protocol safety, treasury stewardship/downside protection, evidence quality/reproducibility, public-benefit ecosystem growth, then execution speed/social consensus momentum. [doctrine: values_hierarchy.md]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Values Hierarchy order.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine says TreasuryWithdrawals without a complete dossier are scored directionally with an explicit -0.10 caution penalty under the current soft gate, and incomplete diligence is recorded as explicit uncertainty. [doctrine: treasury_spending_doctrine.md]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Dossier Gate Posture soft gate language.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine rejects first-come-first-served budget models and uses a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework. [doctrine: treasury_spending_doctrine.md]
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Treasury Spending Doctrine core position.

verification.summary: All 27 dossier facts are supported by the supplied source material. No contradictions or invented material specifics were found.
